name: Domatosoupa
recipeIngredient: 1 large Mild Spanish onion, finely chopped
1 medium Leek, washed and finely chopped
1 1/4 lb Tomatoes, skinned, seeded, and finely chopped
3 Sun-dried tomatoes, snipped up and soaked in boiling water for 30 minutes
1 1/2 cup Thick yogurt or sour cream
1 small Hot dried chili pepper, OR...
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the herb broth: In a saucepan, combine all ingredients with 5 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain before using.
2. To make the soup, in a heavy-bottomed pot, sauté onion and leek in olive oil until translucent.
3. Add chopped tomatoes. In a mortar and pestle, pound sun-dried tomatoes with their liquid to a paste, then add to the pot with sugar. Cook over moderately high heat for 2-3 minutes.
4. Stir in garlic, grated orange zest, and mint, and cook another 2-3 minutes.
5. Gradually add the herb broth, cover, and allow soup to simmer for 30-45 minutes longer. Soup may be prepared ahead of time up to this point.
6. In a bowl, mix yogurt and flour until smooth. Gradually add hot soup, stirring constantly. Add another spoonful of soup, then slowly add yogurt mixture to the soup pot, stirring all the while.
7. Continue cooking soup over gentle heat, stirring, being careful not to let the mixture curdle. Adjust seasonings, then add fresh parsley. Serve immediately with fresh crusty bread, a green salad, olive and feta cheese as suitable accompaniments.
